channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: One of the biggest flaws exploited by Salt Typhoon hackers has had a patch available for years
item.description: The group behind the notorious Treasury hack regularly exploits this vulnerability, but it had a simple fix.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Todo sobre los CIO 100 Awards Spain 2024
item.description: En la última edición de los CIO 100 Awards celebrados en España (el 11 de diciembre de 2024) resultaron premiados los ejecutivos Juan Antonio Relaño, de Bosch, y Olga Forné, de Abertis, como el mejor CIO y CISO del año, mientras que las distinciones a la mejor empresa y administración pública recaye
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Outsmart hackers who are out to steal your identity
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers tips on how to protect your identity: Be vigilant, monitor accounts, use smart security, know theft response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Another gloomy Nvidia RTX 5000 rumor suggests next-gen GPUs will be price hiked over the MSRP in many cases, and I’m getting worried now
item.description: Between potentially hiked pricing and graphics card stock worries, is this leaving AMD an opportunity to really pile the pressure on?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hackers use GenAI to attack more frequently and effectively
item.description: Generative AI is being used in cybersecurity solutions and threats, report claims - so how can your business cope?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Frustrated with today’s ‘attention economy’? You’re really going to hate what comes next
item.description: In the 1990s, the internet was a bit of a wonderland. It was new and liberating and largely free of corporate and government influence. Thirty years later, I don’t think any of us would describe the internet this way.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Allstate sued for allegedly tracking and selling 45M Americans' location data
item.description: Many apps unrelated to location still ask users for tracking permission, and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit seeking to end this alleged practice by Allstate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Last 4 escaped monkeys are captured in South Carolina after months on the loose
item.description: Officials say they have recaptured the final four monkeys who escaped two months ago from a South Carolina compound that breeds them for medical research.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Corporate One enables immediate payments with data orchestration hub
item.description: Corporate One Federal Credit Union, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, is a non-profit that’s provided investment, funding, and payment solutions to their members for nearly 75 years.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: JetBrains launches AI coding agent
item.description: JetBrains has launched an AI coding agent, named Junie, with the ability to quickly solve coding tasks that otherwise could take hours, according to JetBrains.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: World2Meet redefine la asistencia al viajero con un innovador avatar hiperrealista
item.description: En el marco de la celebración de la edición 2025 de Fitur, la capital vuelve a convertirse en una suerte de escaparate de alcance mundial para las empresas dedicadas en cuerpo y alma al sector hospitality.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nearly 250 million children missed school last year because of extreme weather, UNICEF says
item.description: The United Nations Children's Fund says at least 242 million children in 85 countries had their schooling interrupted last year because of heatwaves, cyclones, flooding and other extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palo Alto Networks releases QRNG API framework
item.description: Cybersecurity company Palo Alto Networks has released the Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) Open API framework, enabling users to prepare for quantum security threats.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump says he will ‘demand’ that interest rates come down, but it won’t be that simple
item.description: President Donald Trump has pledged cheaper prices and lower interest rates, but an economy transformed by the pandemic will make those promises difficult to keep.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN
item.description: Lt. Gen. Paul Stanton, commander of JFHQ-DoDIN, said commanders can better manage risks and focus on defending the applications and data that matters the most. The post Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cisco CEO Robbins on AI: Pressure to deploy is real
item.description: Interest in AI has exploded over the last two or three years, but enterprises are only just beginning to think about how they’re going to take advantage of it, Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins told the audience at the company’s recent AI Summit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: "Those accounts are managed by the White House so with a new administration, the content on those Pages changes," Andy Stone wrote.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Intel needs to do to get its mojo back
item.description: While 2024 was a banner year for some chip companies – notably Nvidia and Broadcom – Intel had a year it would rather forget. Another CEO was forced out, it lost market share, and its stock dropped 60% during a year that was strong for semiconductors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech titans sound off on Trump's AI project
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For the new Congress, an AI proposal from the last Congress
item.description: Members of the bipartisan House Task Force on AI want to ensure U.S. leadership while mitigating the threats to privacy and safety. The post For the new Congress, an AI proposal from the last Congress first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Stargate Project launched for OpenAI AI infrastructure
item.description: In a venture unveiled by Pres. Donald Trump on January 21, companies including SoftBank, Oracle, OpenAI, and MGX are funding a new AI company, called the Stargate Project, to invest $500 billion to build AI infrastructure for OpenAI in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 3 Python web frameworks for beautiful front ends
item.description: Python has long had a presence as a language for server-side frameworks, with support for most every project size or use case. But it’s historically been confined to the back end—there’s no real culture of writing Python for creating front-end, client-side code. At least not yet.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump open to Elon Musk buying TikTok
item.description: TikTok restored service to U.S. users Sunday afternoon and credited Trump for pushing to keep the app operational.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
